description: You are a materials scientist analyzing a eutectic alloy microstructure image (~/Fiji_Data/raw/alloy/AuPbSn40.jpg) to quantitatively discriminate between Phase A (brighter, finer-textured) and Phase B (darker, coarser-textured) using texture features. Define at least three square ROIs (~80-120 pixels per side) for each phase, naming them PhaseA_1, PhaseA_2, PhaseA_3 and PhaseB_1, PhaseB_2, PhaseB_3 respectively. Save the ROI set to ~/Fiji_Data/results/texture/rois.zip. Compute texture features (such as ASM, Contrast, Correlation, IDM, and Entropy via GLCM, or statistical measures from variance-filtered images) for each ROI. Save these measurements to ~/Fiji_Data/results/texture/glcm_measurements.csv, ensuring the file includes a header row and at least six rows of data. Generate an annotated overlay image showing the ROI locations on the microstructure and save it to ~/Fiji_Data/results/texture/annotated_microstructure.png. Write a summary report to ~/Fiji_Data/results/texture/texture_report.txt containing the Mean ± StdDev of each texture feature for both phases, an identification of the feature that best discriminates between them, and a conclusion on whether texture analysis successfully distinguishes the two phases.
